response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced polyglot engineer with strong testing culture and some low-latency systems background, but he does not meet the role's core mandatory requirements. His hands-on C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2007-2008 plus a thesis project, far short of the required 5-10 years of Windows C++ development. His database experience is MySQL/MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and his financial exposure (RIDE Capital) was TypeScript/Node-based fintech, not capital markets or fixed income. The skill mismatch is too large for a specialized C++ capital-markets consulting engagement.
strengths: Long, diverse engineering career with low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang at Sociomantic)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and community teaching
Some financial-sector exposure through investment-platform work at RIDE Capital
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2007-2008), versus a mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL and MongoDB
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain knowledge; no Summit platform exposure

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth for mandatory requirement
Missing fixed income / capital markets domain expertise
Transferable low-latency systems and engineering rigor
